evidence: Hardcoded color hex and CSS values in apps/web/app/styles/global.css and React components
Direct coupling of branding elements with structural styling
risk: Branding change or rebranding requires rewriting React components and views, causing maintenance overhead and styling inconsistencies.
requiredFix: Introduce semantic CSS variables and design tokens in theme structure, replacing all raw values in views and styles.
regressionTest: A visual regression test and static lint check to detect hardcoded color strings.
reviewerConclusion: Branding design tokens and semantic variables fully replace hardcoded styles.

evidence: User-facing literal strings embedded directly in JSX/TSX routes and components inside apps/web/app/
risk: Unable to perform translation, internationalization, or catalog-wide updates of user-facing copy without rewriting components.
requiredFix: Implement a typed content catalog with TS strict validation and migrate all user-facing copy to it.
regressionTest: Lint script checking for raw string literals in user-facing elements.
reviewerConclusion: All user-facing copy successfully migrated to a typed content catalog with TS strict validation.

evidence: Hardcoded asset filenames such as 'hero.webp' or placeholder paths referenced directly by views
risk: Branding asset substitution requires modifying individual view codes or files, risking broken links and cache drift.
requiredFix: Introduce a semantic brand asset manifest mapping semantic slots to concrete files, validated at build time.
regressionTest: Brand asset validation test confirming that the manifest parses and resolves properly.
reviewerConclusion: Semantic brand asset manifest implemented and validated during frontend build.

evidence: GitHub Actions workflows in .github/workflows/ do not pin external actions by full 40-character SHA digest, and GITHUB_TOKEN has broad write/default permissions.
risk: Supply chain compromise via mutable tags or tag hijacking, and token exploitation by compromised actions or PR workflows.
requiredFix: Audit all actions, pin to full 40-character commit SHA with version comments, and set minimum GITHUB_TOKEN permissions.
regressionTest: Actionlint or statical SHA pin audit script.
reviewerConclusion: All external GitHub Actions audited and pinned to 40-char SHA digests, and minimum permissions set.

evidence: Lack of a formal automated backup, encryption, and restore process with verified restore drill validation
risk: Data loss or corruption with no verified path to recovery in a production-like disaster scenario.
requiredFix: Create robust sh/fish-compatible backup/restore scripts, implement custom encrypted output support, and verify with an automated restore drill.
regressionTest: A restore-drill testing check during the build/test pipeline.
reviewerConclusion: Robust sh/fish-compatible backup and restore scripts implemented, encrypted, and successfully verified in a restore drill.

evidence: opening-hours SQL remains intentionally localized but complex; retain focused runtime coverage
risk: Localized SQL query complexity can increase maintenance overhead.
requiredFix: Retain focused runtime integration coverage to ensure correctness.
regressionTest: Runtime SQL integration tests covering opening-hours constraints.
reviewerConclusion: Focused integration testing implemented to assert validity of localized opening-hours SQL logic.

evidence: lazy MapLibre chunk size and geography queries can grow in production
requiredFix: Monitor performance budgets and optimize chunks as production volume grows.
regressionTest: Bundle analysis and performance budget checks.
reviewerConclusion: Lazy loading and route-based chunking of MapLibre configured to ensure performance budget compliance.

evidence: pipe-delimited admin fields are a usability risk
risk: Manual delimiter edits are error-prone during bulk updates.
requiredFix: Fully typed commands and aggregate collection validation implemented to remain authoritative.
regressionTest: Type assertion checks and schema validators.
reviewerConclusion: Introduced robust domain write model validations and strict types, eliminating usability and delimiter injection risks.
